Title: User Access Process - First Time Access
Preconditions: Application is launched for the first time.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. System checks if it's the first access.
3. System prompts for registration.
4. User completes registration process.
5. User reaches the home page.
Expected Result: User successfully registers and lands on the home page.

Title: User Access Process - Already Registered User
Preconditions: Application is launched by a previously registered user.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. System checks if it's the first access.
3. System prompts for login.
4. User enters valid login credentials.
5. System verifies credentials with the server.
6. User reaches the home page.
Expected Result: User successfully logs in and lands on the home page.

Title: Start Journey Process - Ongoing Journey
Preconditions: User is currently on a journey.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the home page.
2. System checks if a journey is ongoing.
3. System prompts to start the journey.
4. User confirms to start the journey.
5. System checks if the journey is completed.
6. System calculates emissions.
Expected Result: Journey is started, completed, and emissions are calculated.

Title: Change Section Process - No Ongoing Journey
Preconditions: User is not on a journey.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the home page.
2. System checks if a journey is ongoing.
3. System prompts to change sections.
4. User confirms to change sections.
5. User selects a horizontal menu.
Expected Result: User successfully changes sections via the horizontal menu.

Title: Stay on Home Page - No Ongoing Journey and No Section Change
Preconditions: User is not on a journey and does not want to change sections.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the home page.
2. System checks if a journey is ongoing.
3. System prompts to change sections.
4. User declines to change sections.
Expected Result: User stays on the home page.

Title: View Weekly Report - Yes Option Selected
Preconditions: User is viewing statistics.
Test Steps:
1. User views statistics.
2. System asks if the user wants to view a weekly report.
3. User confirms yes.
4. System generates a weekly report.
5. User returns to the statistics menu.
Expected Result: Weekly report is generated and user returns to statistics menu.

Title: View Weekly Report - No Option Selected
Preconditions: User is viewing statistics.
Test Steps:
1. User views statistics.
2. System asks if the user wants to view a weekly report.
3. User confirms no.
4. User remains in the statistics menu.
Expected Result: User remains in the statistics menu without generating a report.

Title: Update Account - Yes Option Selected
Preconditions: User is viewing their personal data.
Test Steps:
1. User views personal data.
2. System asks if the user wants to update the account.
3. User confirms yes.
4. User inputs new data.
5. System updates the data.
Expected Result: Account data is updated successfully.

Title: Logout Process - Yes Option Selected
Preconditions: User is viewing their personal data.
Test Steps:
1. User views personal data.
2. System asks if the user wants to logout.
3. User confirms yes.
4. System logs out the user.
Expected Result: User is logged out successfully.

Title: Change Section - Yes Option Selected
Preconditions: User is viewing their personal data.
Test Steps:
1. User views personal data.
2. System asks if the user wants to change sections.
3. User confirms yes.
4. User selects a horizontal menu.
Expected Result: User successfully changes sections via the horizontal menu.

Title: Successful Login Process
Preconditions: User has valid login credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login process.
2. Application sends credentials to the server.
3. Server verifies data with the database.
4. Server compares entered data with stored data.
5. Server confirms successful login to the application.
6. Application informs user of successful login.
Expected Result: User successfully logs in.

Title: Failed Login Process - Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User has invalid login credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login process.
2. Application sends credentials to the server.
3. Server verifies data with the database.
4. Server compares entered data with stored data.
5. Server confirms failed login to the application.
6. Application informs user of failed login.
Expected Result: User receives a failed login message.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Successful Route Calculation
Preconditions: User requests route calculation.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server calculates and sends data to the application.
4. Application adds the route.
5. Server returns emissions data.
6. Application returns CO2 emissions to the user.
Expected Result: CO2 emissions are successfully calculated and displayed to the user.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Invalid Route Details
Preconditions: User requests route calculation with invalid details.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server attempts to calculate and send data but fails due to invalid input.
4. Application displays an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating invalid route details.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - No Route Calculation Requested
Preconditions: User does not request route calculation.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application does not initiate route calculation.
3. Application displays a message indicating no route calculation was requested.
Expected Result: User receives a message indicating no route calculation was performed.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Server Failure During Calculation
Preconditions: Server fails during route calculation.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server attempts to calculate and send data but fails.
4. Application displays an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating a failure in route calculation.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Database Failure During Verification
Preconditions: Database fails during route calculation verification.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server attempts to verify data with the database but fails.
4. Application displays an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating a failure in database verification.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Successful Route Calculation with Edge Cases
Preconditions: User requests route calculation with edge case details.
Test Data: Edge case route details (e.g., very short or long routes)
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server calculates and sends data to the application.
4. Application adds the route.
5. Server returns emissions data.
6. Application returns CO2 emissions to the user.
Expected Result: CO2 emissions are successfully calculated and displayed to the user, even for edge case routes.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - No Data Found in Database
Preconditions: No data found in the database for route calculation.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server attempts to verify data with the database but finds no matching records.
4. Application displays an error message.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating no data was found in the database.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Duplicate Route Calculation Requests
Preconditions: User makes duplicate route calculation requests.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server calculates and sends data to the application.
4. Application adds the route.
5. Server returns emissions data.
6. User dispatches another identical request.
7. Application processes the second request as a duplicate.
8. Application returns CO2 emissions to the user.
Expected Result: Second route calculation request is processed as a duplicate, and emissions are displayed to the user.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Server Timeout During Calculation
Preconditions: Server times out during route calculation.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server attempts to calculate and send data but times out.
4. Application displays a timeout error message.
Expected Result: User receives a timeout error message indicating the server timed out during route calculation.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Network Error During Data Transfer
Preconditions: Network error occurs during data transfer.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server attempts to calculate and send data but encounters a network error.
4. Application displays a network error message.
Expected Result: User receives a network error message indicating a problem occurred during data transfer.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Successful Route Calculation with Large Data Set
Preconditions: User requests route calculation with a large data set.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server calculates and sends data to the application.
4. Application adds the route.
5. Server returns emissions data.
6. Application returns CO2 emissions to the user.
Expected Result: CO2 emissions are successfully calculated and displayed to the user, even for large data sets.

Title: Calculate Emissions Process - Successful Route Calculation with Minimal Data Set
Preconditions: User requests route calculation with minimal data set.
Test Steps:
1. User dispatches a request to the application.
2. Application starts route calculation.
3. Server calculates and sends data to the application.
4. Application adds the route.
5. Server returns emissions data.
6. Application returns CO2 emissions to the user.
Expected Result: CO2 emissions are successfully calculated and displayed to the user, even for minimal data sets.
